name: Horizon Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank operating in Nebraska with 5 locations offering checking, savings, and specialized lending including Low Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) financing.
description_long:
Horizon Bank is a regional FDIC-insured bank headquartered in Nebraska with over 20 years of experience in specialized commercial lending. The bank operates five branch locations across Nebraska (Waverly, Superior, McCook, and Springfield) and serves both consumer and commercial banking needs with full FDIC deposit protection backed by the U.S. Government.

The bank's primary service offerings include standard banking products (checking, savings, online banking) alongside specialized commercial lending expertise. Their core differentiator is Low Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) financing, where they provide construction loans, term financing, tax incremental financing, and bridge loans for affordable housing development projects. They maintain an active portfolio of affordable housing projects across multiple states including South Dakota, Kansas, Iowa, Missouri, Arkansas, Texas, Oklahoma, Colorado, Illinois, and Wyoming.

Horizon Bank distinguishes itself through direct access to decision-makers for LIHTC transactions and claims expertise in navigating complex regulatory requirements in affordable housing finance. The bank has established relationships with housing developers and tax credit syndicators, positioning themselves as specialists in timing-sensitive LIHTC projects. They offer both digital banking (online and mobile access with after-hours support) and traditional branch banking with varying lobby and drive-through hours across locations.

As a regional Nebraska bank, Horizon Bank is best suited for businesses and nonprofits involved in affordable housing development, existing customers in their service areas seeking community banking relationships, and consumers requiring FDIC-insured deposit accounts. However, their service territory is geographically limited to Nebraska branches, and specialized LIHTC financing may not be relevant to most consumer banking customers. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Low Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) construction financing
Tax incremental financing for affordable housing projects
Bridge loan financing for housing development
Lost card and fraud reporting services
Project portfolio management and advisory for affordable housing
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing of U.S. Government
20+ years specialized experience in Low Income Housing Tax Credit financing
Direct access to decision-makers for LIHTC loan approval and structuring
Active portfolio across 10 states demonstrates established relationships with housing developers
Multiple Nebraska branch locations with extended hours including Saturday availability
Online and mobile banking with dedicated after-hours digital support (855-812-6810)
Fraud protection with dedicated lost card/fraud reporting line (888-263-3370)
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only 5 branches in Nebraska; not suitable for customers outside service area
LIHTC financing expertise is specialized and irrelevant to most retail consumers
Website provides no information on deposit account fees, interest rates, or consumer lending products
No details on personal loan, credit card, or mortgage offerings despite being a bank
Limited business hours compared to modern digital banks (most locations close by 5pm weekdays)
best_for: Affordable housing developers and nonprofits seeking LIHTC construction and permanent financing
Tax credit syndicators working on low-income housing projects in Horizon Bank's service area
Consumers and businesses in Nebraska seeking FDIC-insured community bank relationships
Borrowers requiring direct decision-maker access for complex housing finance transactions
diagnosis: Horizon Bank is best for affordable housing developers, syndicators, and nonprofits needing specialized LIHTC financing with direct lender access, or Nebraska residents seeking traditional community banking with FDIC protection. The main caveat is that this is a regional bank with limited geographic reach (Nebraska only) and no prominent consumer lending products; most retail customers should look for banks with broader service areas or product offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
